articles.title: Heatwave alert: Nabaranagpur joins Bolangir, Subarnapur, Kalahandi, and Boudh district to close schools and colleges - Kalinga TV
articles.search_term: heatwave (Angul OR Balangir OR Balasore OR Bargarh OR Bhadrak OR Boudh OR Cuttack OR Deogarh OR Dhenkanal OR Gajapati OR Ganjam OR Jagatsinghapur OR Jajpur OR Jharsuguda OR Kalahandi OR Kandhamal OR Kendrapara OR "Kendujhar (Keonjhar)" OR Khordha OR Koraput OR Malkangiri OR Mayurbhanj OR Nabarangpur OR Nayagarh OR Nuapada OR Puri OR Rayagada OR Sambalpur OR Sonepur OR Sundargarh) Odisha
articles.full_text:
Heatwave alert: Nabaranagpur joins Bolangir, Subarnapur, Kalahandi, and Boudh district to close schools and colleges
Four districts of Odisha has announced the closure of all Anganwadi Centres (AWCS), Schools, Colleges, Degree Colleges, and Technical Institutions until further notice.
Bhubaneswar: Hot and humid weather is being experienced across Odisha due to the ongoing severe heatwave. The heatwave makes it difficult for people, especially children and elders to venture outside during the day time.
In view of the prevailing heat wave conditions, several districts of the state has announced the closure of all Anganwadi Centres (AWCS), Schools, Colleges, Degree Colleges, KVS/OVS/JNV, and Technical Institutions until further notice.
Earlier, four districts including Bolangir, Subarnapur, Kalahandi, and Boudh has declared to close schools and colleges. On Saturday, Nabarangapur joined them and declared that all Anganwadi Centres (AWCS), Schools, Colleges, Degree Colleges, KVS/OVS/JNV, and Technical Institutions from April 20 until further notice.
Advertisement
The schools and colleges in Bolangir and Kalahandi will remain closed till April 21. Meanwhile, Boudh and Subarnapur collectors have announced to closed the schools and colleges till April 22.
However, the examinations scheduled during this period shall continue and may be rescheduled in morning hours. The Heads of Institutions have been ordered to ensure proper provision of drinking water facilities and necessary heat-wave safety measures are arranged for students and staff.

articles.title: Heatwave forces five-day closure of schools, colleges in Boudh; exams to continue with precautions - Odisha TV
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Odisha
articles.full_text:
In view of the intensifying heatwave, the Boudh district administration has announced the closure of all schools and colleges for five days, from April 18 to April 22, as soaring temperatures begin to disrupt normal life. The decision has been taken as a precautionary measure to safeguard students from extreme heat conditions.
Despite the suspension of regular classes, academic activities will not come to a complete halt. Authorities have confirmed that ongoing examinations will continue, and they've asked institutions to reschedule them to cooler morning hours to minimise students’ exposure to rising daytime temperatures.
Exams to Continue with Adjustments
Officials emphasised that the closure applies only to classroom teaching and not to examinations. Educational institutions have been asked to schedule exams early in the day so students can complete them before the heat intensifies.
The move aims to strike a balance between ensuring student safety and maintaining the academic schedule without major disruption.
Safety Measures Made Mandatory
District Collector Bibhuti Bhushan Nayak has directed all schools and colleges to ensure adequate arrangements for drinking water and heat protection for both students and staff. Institutions have been asked to remain fully prepared, particularly for those appearing in examinations during this period.

With temperatures continuing to rise, the administration is focused on minimising health risks while ensuring essential academic functions continue. The coming days are expected to be challenging as the region grapples with a severe spell of summer heat.

articles.title: Heatwave forces closure of educational institutions in Nabarangpur - prameyanews.com
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Odisha
articles.full_text:
Nabarangpur, April 18: In view of the intense heatwave sweeping across the district, the administration has ordered the closure of all Anganwadi centres, government and private schools, colleges, and technical institutions from April 20, 2026, until further notice.
Officials said the decision was taken to safeguard students from extreme weather conditions as temperatures continue to soar. However, scheduled examinations will be conducted as planned. Authorities have advised that exams may be rescheduled to morning hours to avoid peak heat.
Institution heads have been directed to ensure adequate arrangements, including the availability of safe drinking water and necessary measures to tackle heat-related illnesses during examination hours.
Earlier closures in Boudh and Bolangir
Prior to Nabarangpur, similar steps had been taken in Boudh and Bolangir districts, where the district administrations ordered the closure of schools due to rising temperatures and heatwave conditions.
In both districts, authorities emphasized student safety amid the harsh summer and directed educational institutions to remain shut until weather conditions improve. Like Nabarangpur, examinations were allowed to continue with precautionary measures, including conducting them during cooler morning hours and ensuring proper hydration facilities for students.
